paper_title: Sampling Protocols for Elevation and Material Collection

methods.description: Process for measuring and reporting elevations of sample sites using handheld barometric digital altimeters and lidar data.

steps.description: Elevation measurements were taken using handheld barometric digital altimeters accurate to ±1 m and zeroed as frequently as possible.
steps.searchable_summary: measure elevation using barometric digital altimeters

steps.description: Measured elevations are reported as meters above or below mean lower low water (AMLLW or BMLLW) or a 0.0 m tide.
steps.searchable_summary: report elevations in AMLLW or BMLLW

steps.description: Lidar data collected in 2017 and 2018 for various islands were delivered with a vertical datum of NAVD88, geoid model GEOID12B, in meters.
steps.searchable_summary: fetch lidar data with NAVD88 datum

methods.description: Procedures for collecting and processing bulk samples of shell-bearing strata, including washing, screening, and dating materials.

steps.description: Bulk samples of shell-bearing strata were collected in gallon-sized resealable plastic bags.
steps.searchable_summary: collect bulk samples in plastic bags

steps.description: Recovered samples were washed and screened through two graduated U.S.A. Standard Testing Sieves: #16, 1.18 mm and #200, 0.075 mm.
steps.searchable_summary: wash and screen samples through sieves

steps.description: Care was taken to date charcoal or wood in addition to shells whenever possible, selecting individual conifer needles or small limbs to minimize 'old wood' errors.
When dating shell material, short-lived species were favored over longer-lived species.
steps.searchable_summary: prefer short-lived species for shell dating

steps.description: If a large shell was the only datable material, only the outermost growth was used.
steps.searchable_summary: use outermost growth of large shells for dating

steps.description: Fish bones recovered at one site were sent for species-level identification.
